title: Best Fishing Spots Smith Mountain Lake | Where to Fish
metaDescription: Find the best fishing spots on Smith Mountain Lake. Local guide Captain Tommy Moore shares where to fish SML for stripers, bass, crappie & catfish year-round.
content:
Best Fishing Spots on Smith Mountain Lake: A Complete Guide

With over 20,000 acres of water and 500 miles of shoreline, figuring out the best fishing spots on Smith Mountain Lake can feel genuinely overwhelming — especially for visiting anglers who don't know the lake's layout. Whether you're planning your first trip or you're a local angler looking to expand your repertoire and discover new water, understanding where to fish SML is the single biggest factor that determines whether you have a great day or a frustrating one. Captain Tommy Moore of SML Wicked Striper Guide Service has spent years systematically mapping the most productive areas of this incredible Virginia fishery.

Understanding Smith Mountain Lake's Geography

Before diving into specific areas, it helps to understand the lake's overall layout and character. Smith Mountain Lake is a reservoir formed by Smith Mountain Dam on the Roanoke River in the Blue Ridge Mountains of Virginia. The lake has two main arms — the Roanoke River arm extending to the northwest and the Blackwater River arm extending to the southwest — that converge near the dam in a distinctive Y-shape.

This geographic diversity is what makes SML such a productive and versatile fishery. Different sections of the lake fish very differently depending on the season, and knowing which areas to target at which times is the core expertise that Captain Tommy brings to every guided trip from his base in Moneta, VA.

Upper Roanoke River Arm

The upper Roanoke arm is shallower and more riverine in character, with visible current during generation periods from upstream. This area excels in several key scenarios:

  • Spring: Stripers push upriver during the pre-spawn migration, creating a natural concentration effect that makes them significantly easier to locate and target. Huge numbers of fish funnel into relatively narrow water.
  • Year-round: Excellent largemouth bass habitat exists along laydowns, boat docks, rocky banks, and submerged vegetation throughout this section
  • Fall: Cooling temperatures push bait concentrations into the narrower upper reaches, attracting all predator species and creating feeding frenzies in confined water
  • Crappie: Standing timber and brush piles in the upper Roanoke arm hold outstanding crappie populations, especially productive in spring

Upper Blackwater River Arm

The Blackwater arm offers similar characteristics to the Roanoke arm but with its own unique structure, depth profiles, and seasonal patterns. This section tends to receive less fishing pressure than the Roanoke side, which can translate to less wary fish and more willing biters. Crappie fishing is particularly strong throughout the Blackwater section, with numerous brush piles and standing timber that hold fish throughout the year. The slightly more stained water in this arm can also extend the morning bite window longer into the day.

Mid-Lake Region — The Sweet Spot

Where the arms begin to widen and deepen, you'll find the mid-lake region — arguably the most versatile and consistently productive section of Smith Mountain Lake for striper fishing. This area features:

  • Numerous underwater humps and ridges that hold stripers year-round regardless of season
  • Creek arm mouths that create bait-concentrating current seams and feeding zones
  • A diverse mix of deep and shallow water within short distances, allowing quick adjustments
  • Less overall fishing pressure than the easily-accessible upper sections near popular marinas
  • Major channel bends from the old river bed that create ledges and depth transitions stripers love

Captain Tommy spends a significant amount of his fishing time in the mid-lake region because it offers the most consistent striper action across all four seasons. When patterns are in transition and other areas are hit-or-miss, the mid-lake almost always produces something.

Main Lake Basin Near the Dam

The deepest and widest section of Smith Mountain Lake sits near Smith Mountain Dam itself. Depths exceed 200 feet in some areas, and the open-water characteristics create unique fishing opportunities that don't exist in the narrower upper sections:

  • Summer: Deep thermocline fishing for suspended stripers in the 30- to 50-foot range — fish stack up in the oxygen-rich thermocline zone in massive numbers
  • Winter: Schools of trophy-class stripers concentrate in deep main-lake channels and along steep bluff walls near the dam — some of the biggest fish of the year come from this area
  • Year-round: Trolling the main lake basin covers water efficiently and contacts roaming fish that patrol open-water areas following bait schools
  • Deep structure: Submerged humps, ridges, and old road beds near the dam create outstanding deep-water holding structure

Points, Peninsulas, and Transitions

Smith Mountain Lake's 500 miles of shoreline create countless points and peninsulas that extend into deeper water. These are natural ambush points for stripers — places where fish can hold in the comfort of deep water but quickly move shallow to intercept bait traveling along the shoreline. The best points share common characteristics: a gradual taper with rocks or hard bottom, quick access to deep water (30+ feet) nearby, and proximity to a creek channel, river bend, or other current-creating feature.

Marinas, Docks, and Man-Made Structure

While not traditional "fishing spots" in the classic sense, marina areas and dock-lined coves throughout Smith Mountain Lake hold surprising numbers of fish. Dock lights attract baitfish at night, the shade provided by covered boat slips holds fish during sunny midday hours, and the pilings themselves create mini-structure that fish relate to. These areas are especially productive for largemouth bass and crappie throughout the year, with occasional stripers cruising through to ambush concentrated bait.

Why a Local Guide Is the Ultimate Advantage

The honest truth is that even detailed spot descriptions like these can only take you so far. Smith Mountain Lake's fishing changes daily based on weather fronts, water temperature shifts, bait movements, generation schedules from Leesville Dam, wind patterns, and a dozen other variables. What's red-hot productive on Monday might be completely dead by Thursday as conditions evolve.

Captain Tommy is on the water multiple days every week, constantly updating his pattern knowledge, checking spots, and adjusting his approach in real-time. That current, real-time intelligence is something no fishing report, forum post, YouTube video, or blog article can provide. It's the main reason hiring a guide at Smith Mountain Lake dramatically increases your odds of success.

Ready to fish the best spots on Smith Mountain Lake with the guide who knows them intimately? Book your trip with Captain Tommy Moore or call (434) 610-3255.

title: Smith Mountain Lake Fishing Vacation | Plan Your Trip
metaDescription: Plan the perfect Smith Mountain Lake fishing vacation. Lodging, dining, activities & guided fishing trips in Virginia's Blue Ridge Mountains with Captain Tommy.
content:
Plan Your Smith Mountain Lake Fishing Vacation

A Smith Mountain Lake fishing vacation combines world-class striper fishing with the natural beauty of Virginia's Blue Ridge Mountains for an experience that satisfies anglers and their families alike. Whether you're planning a fishing trip to Virginia for the first time or returning to one of the East Coast's finest freshwater destinations, this comprehensive guide covers everything you need to know — from booking your guided trip with Captain Tommy Moore to finding the best lodging, dining, and activities in the Smith Mountain Lake area.

Getting to Smith Mountain Lake

Smith Mountain Lake is located in Bedford and Franklin Counties in southwestern Virginia, centered around the town of Moneta, VA 24121. Despite its peaceful, mountain-retreat feel, the lake is surprisingly accessible from major population centers:

  • 30 minutes southeast of Roanoke, VA (nearest major city with full services)
  • 60 minutes south of Lexington, VA (home of VMI and Washington & Lee University)
  • 3 hours southwest of Richmond, VA (Virginia's capital)
  • 4 hours southwest of Washington, D.C. and the Northern Virginia suburbs
  • 4.5 hours west of Virginia Beach and the Hampton Roads area
  • 3.5 hours north of Charlotte, NC

The closest commercial airport is Roanoke-Blacksburg Regional Airport (ROA), which offers connections through major hubs including Charlotte, Chicago, and Atlanta. From the airport, it's an easy 30-minute drive through beautiful Virginia countryside to the lake. Most visitors drive, which gives you flexibility to explore the area at your own pace.

Lodging Options Around the Lake

Smith Mountain Lake offers accommodations for every budget, group size, and style preference:

Lakefront Vacation Rentals: The most popular option for fishing vacations, and for good reason. Hundreds of lakefront homes and cabins are available through rental agencies, VRBO, and Airbnb. Many include private docks where Captain Tommy can pick you up directly for your fishing trip — no marina trip required. Having a full kitchen lets you cook your catch for dinner, which is a highlight of any fishing vacation.

Resorts and Hotels: The Mariners Landing resort and other hotels around the lake provide comfortable stays with lake access, pools, restaurants, fitness centers, and organized activities. These are ideal for families who want structured amenities beyond fishing, or for corporate groups that need meeting space alongside their fishing experience.

Campgrounds: Smith Mountain Lake State Park offers camping with lake access, hiking trails, and a swimming beach — a budget-friendly option that puts you right in nature. Several private campgrounds in the area offer full RV hookups and tent sites with shower facilities.

Bed and Breakfasts: Charming B&Bs dot the surrounding countryside, offering a more intimate Virginia experience with home-cooked breakfasts that provide the perfect fuel for a morning on the water with Captain Tommy.

Building Your Fishing Itinerary

How many days should you fish? Here's Captain Tommy's recommendation based on trip length:

Weekend trip (2-3 days): Book one full-day guided trip and spend the other day exploring the lake on a rented pontoon, relaxing at your rental, or visiting local attractions. One solid guided day gives you a quality fishing experience and leaves time for other activities.

Week-long vacation (5-7 days): Book 2-3 guided fishing days spaced throughout the week with rest and activity days in between. This gives you fishing days mixed with relaxation, exploration, and family time. Captain Tommy can target different species, techniques, or areas of the lake each day to keep the experience fresh and diverse.

Dedicated fishing trip (3-5 days): For serious anglers who live and breathe fishing, 3-5 consecutive guided days allows you to really dial in the seasonal patterns, learn the lake's personality, explore multiple areas, and maximize your chances at landing a true trophy striper. This is how memories — and personal records — are made.

Beyond Fishing: Things to Do at Smith Mountain Lake

Smith Mountain Lake and the surrounding Blue Ridge region offer plenty of activities for non-fishing days or for family members who'd rather stay onshore:

  • Smith Mountain Lake State Park: Excellent hiking trails through mountain forests, swimming beach, kayak and paddleboard rentals, and picnic areas with stunning lake views
  • Booker T. Washington National Monument: The historic birthplace of the famous educator and leader, located just minutes from the lake — a meaningful and educational visit
  • Blue Ridge Parkway: America's favorite scenic drive is nearby, with jaw-dropping mountain overlooks, hiking trails, and the historic Peaks of Otter Lodge for lunch with a view
  • Wineries and Craft Breweries: Several local vineyards and craft breweries offer tastings with Blue Ridge Mountain views — a perfect afternoon activity after a morning fishing trip
  • Water Sports: Swimming, kayaking, paddleboarding, tubing, and wakeboarding are all popular on the lake during warm months
  • Golf: Multiple courses in the area offer challenging play with spectacular mountain scenery
  • Shopping and Antiques: The small towns surrounding the lake feature charming shops, antique stores, and local artisan galleries

Dining Around Smith Mountain Lake

The SML area has a growing and increasingly diverse restaurant scene with options ranging from casual lakeside grills serving burgers and fried fish to upscale dining with wine lists and mountain views. Fresh seafood, authentic Virginia barbecue, farm-to-table restaurants using local ingredients, and classic American fare are all within easy reach. Several lakeside restaurants are accessible by boat, making lunch stops during non-guided days both convenient and uniquely fun.

Best Time to Visit for a Fishing Vacation

Smith Mountain Lake is a legitimate four-season destination, but each season offers a distinctly different experience:

  • Spring (April-May): Mild weather, dogwood and redbud blooms, excellent striper fishing, fewer crowds, moderate lodging rates
  • Summer (June-August): Peak vacation season — warm weather, full lake activities, vibrant community events, and the most on-water entertainment options
  • Fall (September-November): Stunning Blue Ridge foliage that draws visitors from across the region, outstanding fishing, comfortable temperatures, and reduced crowds
  • Winter (December-February): Quiet and serene atmosphere, some of the best trophy striper fishing of the year, lowest lodging rates, and virtually no boat traffic

For the best combination of fishing quality and overall vacation experience, Captain Tommy recommends late April through early June, or September through October. These shoulder seasons offer excellent fishing, pleasant weather, and fewer crowds than peak summer.

Start Planning Your Trip Today

The first and most important step in planning your Smith Mountain Lake fishing vacation is securing your guided trip dates with Captain Tommy Moore. His calendar fills up — especially during peak season — so booking early ensures you get your preferred dates.

Book your guided fishing trip with Captain Tommy Moore or call (434) 610-3255 to discuss dates, trip options, and personalized local recommendations. He's genuinely happy to help you plan the perfect Virginia fishing getaway. Check our pricing page for current rates and trip options.
